thesis: Scanner-based intraday long setups should improve when treated as selection-plus-entry systems on approved Mag7 ex-GOOG universes and gated by ETF proxy risk-on context, while validated daily controls remain in the round.

benchmark.purpose: Primary passive benchmark on the common 2025-01-02 to 2026-03-06 sample

universe.name: mag7_intraday_context_v1
universe.selection_rule: Approved Mag7 ex-GOOG large caps plus ETF context proxies, split into core, all, and high-beta buckets

carry_forward_findings: iter_011: MSFT bsl_reversal_intraday was the only clear intraday branch that beat its same-symbol passive baseline.
iter_011: AAPL gap_reversal_intraday deserves secondary follow-up, but it still lagged passive AAPL.
iter_011: Intraday pilots must report activation rate; score alone is misleading for sparse-trigger families.
Discretionary BSL notes indicate large caps and risk-on context matter, so this round adds explicit ETF proxy context filters.

candidates.hypothesis: Passive VOO remains the common benchmark for excess-return comparisons.
notes: Benchmark candidate.

candidates.hypothesis: QQQ/VOO/GLD 126/7 remains the strongest non-leveraged cross-family control.
notes: Carryover control from iter_010.

candidates.hypothesis: TQQQ/VOO/GLD 126/7 at 0.75x remains the leading leveraged deployment hypothesis.
notes: Carryover leveraged control from iter_010.

candidates.hypothesis: GLD 18/110 + 189d remains the best active single-asset trend control.
notes: Carryover control from iter_010.
